rationale: Has NLP/transformers production work at product companies (CRED, Saarthi.ai, Vedantu) and Hugging Face transformers experience, but top skills skew toward Object Detection and TTS rather than IR/retrieval, with no evidence of vector databases, hybrid search, or ranking evaluation metrics (NDCG/MAP).

Career history consists of non-technical roles (content writing, customer support, accounting) at fictional honeypot companies (Initech, Dunder Mifflin) and Wipro, with zero ML or engineering experience.
Graphic designer and business analyst at TCS (IT services) and Acme Corp with no ML, NLP, or information retrieval experience whatsoever.
Entirely non-technical career (accountant, sales executive, operations) at fictional honeypot companies Wayne Enterprises and Initech, with no engineering or ML work.
Only role is enterprise cloud software sales at TCS with a $1.8M ARR quota — no engineering, ML, or retrieval experience of any kind.
Sole employer is Wipro in an enterprise sales capacity; mechanical engineer title with no ML, NLP, or backend engineering substance in the profile.

Solid backend/data engineering background at product companies (Vedantu, PolicyBazaar) with Python, FastAPI, and Spark, but no production embeddings, vector databases, or ranking evaluation framework experience — purely data infrastructure rather than ML/IR.

Production ML at product companies (Razorpay, PharmEasy) with Milvus (vector DB) at advanced level, RAG experience, XGBoost usage, and tier_1 education (MIT + IIIT Bangalore), but at 4.2 years lacks explicit ranking evaluation metric ownership (NDCG/MAP) and production hybrid retrieval deployment.

Predominantly IT-services career (HCL 46 months, Mindtree) doing DevOps/cloud/QA work with only self-learner ML exposure and a small RAG side project; Information Retrieval listed as an intermediate skill but with no production IR system evidence.

Majority of career at Tech Mahindra (49 months, IT services) doing Java/Spring Boot backend work, with Initech (fictional company) as the other employer and no production ML or IR experience despite MLflow listed as an advanced skill.
